name: Pear, Fig Leaf and Blackcurrant Sage
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_3793_parsed
Name: Pear, Fig Leaf and Blackcurrant Sage

Ingredients:
- 4 litres Whole Milk
- 5g Ascorbic Acid
- 2kg Pears
- 2g Ascorbic Acid
- 3% Calcium Hydroxide
- 5% Caster Sugar
- Whey (from 4 litres of milk)
- 5% Sugar
- Pear
- 700g Whole Milk
- 300g Double Cream
- 70g Sugar
- 105g Egg Yolk
- Base (10% total weight of anglaise)

Steps:
1. Heat milk to 93 degrees.
2. Add ascorbic acid and leave milk to split for 2 hours.
3. Pass off whey and discard the curds.
4. Peel and trim pears keeping skin and trim separate.
5. Prepare a lye solution bath for 2 hours.
6. After 2 hours, wash pears thoroughly and put into a vacuum bag with 45g 60 degree syrup.
7. Reduce until pears become glassy and are soft.
8. Take out of water and dry for 8 hours until skin is dry.
9. Reduce whey, sugar and pear until thick toffee.
10. Be careful not to over reduce, if unsure, let it cool to check consistency.
11. Mix milk, cream, sugar and yolk in thermo at 90 degrees, cook at 80 degrees.
12. Blend with butter.
13. Blanche fig leaves, chill in ice bath and infuse.
14. Remove fig leaves from base, check flavour.

Other details:

Peel and trim pears keeping skin and trim separate.

After 2 hours, wash pears thoroughly and put into a vacuum bag with 45g 60 degree syrup.

Be careful not to over reduce, if unsure, let it cool to check consistency.
Mix milk, cream, sugar and yolk in thermo at 90 degrees, cook at 80 degrees.
Blend with butter.
Blanche fig leaves, chill in ice bath and infuse.
Remove fig leaves from base, check flavour.

name: Kombucha de fresa
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_2093_parsed
Name: Kombucha de fresa

Ingredients:
- 200 g agua
- 1 kg fresa
- 100 g azucar

Steps:
1. Limpiar las fresas y retirar el tallo.
2. Triturar en la batidora de azucar y el agua para obtener un pure liquido, colar y agregar al kombucha.
3. Retirar un 80% del liquido antiguo de la bandeja de kombucha y sustituir por liquido nuevo.
4. Guardar el liquido antiguo retirado en parte superior de la bandeja.
5. Almacenar en un lugar avido hasta el momento de su uso o hasta cuando se tenga que volver a alimentar.

Other details:
notes: Megaritz 2016

name: Mackerel, Wasabi
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_3813_parsed
Name: Mackerel, Wasabi

Ingredients:
- 70g salt
- 30g sugar
- 2 mackerel
- 150g rice vinegar
- 10g dried kombu
- 500g light soy sauce
- 500g rice vinegar
- 20g sake
- 10g ginger
- 1.5g fresh wasabi
- Pennywort
- Fresh wasabi

Steps:
1. Clean mackerel and remove fillets.
2. Leave in iced water for 20 mins to remove excess blood.
3. Salt and lightly salt for 2.5 hours. Rinse and dry.
4. Using tweezers, remove rib bones.
5. Remove membrane from the skin and pickle in the liquid 45 minutes.
6. Dry and store in fridge.
7. Infuse the ginger and kombu in the vinegar.
8. Add all the ingredients together and infuse 10 minutes, then pass.
9. Add the pickling liquid to the daikon and cook at 85C for 1 hour.
10. Slice the mackerel into 1.5cm pieces and place on plate.
11. Place 4 strips of the daikon on the plate and garnish with the wasabi and pennywort.

Other details:
extras: Pennywort, Fresh wasabi

name: Caldo de Txipiron
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_2110_parsed
Name: Caldo de Txipiron

Ingredients:
- 20 Kg cebolla cortada en octavos
- 15 kg calamar limpio
- 5 L vino blanco

Steps:
1. Dorar la cebolla en la basculante hasta conseguir un tono dorado muy intenso y una textura de mermelada. El dulzor sera muy intenso al igual que el color.
2. En una olla grande unir el calamar con la cebolla caramelizada.
3. Cubrir el conjunto con el vino y cocer a fuego muy bajo durante 4 horas.
4. Retirar del fuego y colar usando un colador chino (presionar) y posteriormente un colador fino.
5. Colar usando un micro colador.
6. Porcionar en bolsas de 500 ml y congelar.

Other details:
servings: Para 4 L

Steps:

4. Combine all ingredients above and line flat in a vac pack bag.

7. Should maintain a bite and not be mushy.
8. Blast chill.

name: Oblixio
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_3772_parsed
Name: Oblixio

Ingredients:
- Toasted disks
- Neutral caramel
- Smoked cheese

Steps:
1. Check first with a senior member.
2. Spread toasted disks on silpats evenly apart.
3. Either preheat the oven to 220 degrees fan 100 or preheat the grill.
4. Dust the toasted disks with neutral caramel powder to a generous section.
5. Put uncovered in oven or under the salamander until caramelized.
6. Remove from oven and layer one half of the disks with smoked cheese, coriander, and lime.
7. Continue to place the other empty disk on top.
8. Place back in the oven or salamander to melt the cheese and to enable the two to be closed together.

Other details:
finishing_and_plating: ['Place the microcorriander on a folded napkin.', 'Grate the lime and smoked cheese on top.', 'Serve.']

name: Criadillas Cremosas
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_1495_parsed
Name: Criadillas Cremosas

Ingredients:
- 10 hojas de gelatina
- 20 g de globos
- Semillas Chia
- 375 ml de leche de almendra
- 45 g de manteca
- 75 g de aceite de oliva
- 30 g de almendra
- 45 g de pimiento rojo
- 5% de xantana
- 150 ml de agua
- 100 g de criadillas

Steps:
1. Hidratar las hojas de gelatina en agua con hielo.
2. Montar las claras a punto de nieve y añadir las hojas de gelatina.
3. Bañar los globos en el baño de gelatina y rebozarlos en las semillas de chia.
4. Cubrir con papel humedo y dejar en el refrigerador.
5. Hidratar el Metodoch durante 12 horas en 375 ml de leche de almendra.
6. Sofreir los ajos en el aceite.
7. Preparar un roux.
8. Añadir el pimiento y remover hasta que queme.
9. Llevar al fuego y añadir el caldo de gelatina.
10. Añadir la xantana y batir.
11. Servir en sifon con 2 cargas.
12. Limpiar las criadillas y cortarlas en laminas de 1 cm.
13. Asarlas en la parrilla y cortar en dados de 0.5 x 1 cm.

Other details:
servings: Para 20 personas
temperature: Cocinar a 80°C

name: Caldo de Ave
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_2125_parsed
Name: Caldo de Ave

Ingredients:
- 10kg. pollo
- 300g. cebolla
- 200g. tomate
- 100g. puerro
- 100g. mantequilla
- 1l. vino tinto
- 500g. garbanzo hidratado
- 7l. agua

Steps:
1. Reducir el vino tinto hasta obtener 500g.
2. Trocear las piezas de pollo.
3. Dorar el pollo en un horno a 180°C hasta que adquiera un color intenso.
4. Limpiar, pelar y cortar la verdura en mirepoix.
5. En una olla amplia, pochar la verdura con la mantequilla.
6. Anadir los garbanzos hidratados.
7. Anadir el vino tinto reducido.
8. Anadir el agua y llevar a ebullicion.
9. Bajar el fuego al minimo y cocer suavemente durante 90 minutos.
10. Retirar el caldo del fuego y colar.
11. Enfriar en un bano de hielos y refrigerar 8 horas.
12. Retirar la grasa de la superficie del caldo.
13. Envasar el caldo frio en bolsas de vacio grandes en porciones de 2 kg.
14. Congelar las bolsas (deben quedar planas).
15. Abrir las bolsas y descongelar los bloques de caldo sobre panos limpios (gaza para colar caldos). Reservar 2 dias en refrigeracion hasta recuperar todo el liquido.
16. Rectificar el sabor del caldo con reduccion si llegara a ser necesario.
17. Porcionar el caldo en bolsas con 500g. cada una y reservar en congelacion.

Other details:

raw.name: Caldo de Ave
steps: Reducir el vino tinto hasta obtener 500g.
Trocear las piezas de pollo.
Dorar el pollo en un horno a 180°C hasta que adquiera un color intenso.
Limpiar, pelar y cortar la verdura en mirepoix.
En una olla amplia, pochar la verdura con la mantequilla.
Anadir los garbanzos hidratados.
Anadir el vino tinto reducido.
Anadir el agua y llevar a ebullicion.
Bajar el fuego al minimo y cocer suavemente durante 90 minutos.
Retirar el caldo del fuego y colar.
Enfriar en un bano de hielos y refrigerar 8 horas.

Porcionar el caldo en bolsas con 500g. cada una y reservar en congelacion.

name: Importa realmente la verdad?
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_1511_parsed
Name: Importa realmente la verdad?

Ingredients:
- 500g pieles de pollo
- Aceite de girasol
- Caldo de gallina 250ml
- 6% kuzu

Steps:
1. Quitar exceso de grasa de las pieles de pollo con un cuchillo.
2. Deshidratar las pieles completamente estiradas a 65°C durante 12 horas.
3. Recortar las esquinas y reservar en un lugar seco.
4. En una cacerola, calentar el aceite a 180°C.
5. Freir las pieles deshidratadas, recortar las puntas con unas tijeras.
6. Reservar en armario caliente.
7. Disolver el kuzu en el caldo frio.
8. Calentar a fuego medio sin parar de remover hasta que espese.
9. Servir una gota de glase de gallina.
10. Cubrir con la pechuga vacia crujiente.

Other details:
servings: Para 4 personas
temperature: 180°C
cook_time: 12 horas

name: Oyster Strawberry
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_3769_parsed
Name: Oyster Strawberry

Ingredients:
- For the Oyster
- 50g Lemon grass oil
- 300g grapeseed oil
- For the Strawberry
- 150g strawberry puree (sieved)
- 8 drops orange blossom essence
- For the Salt Water
- 70g fine salt
- 2l water
- Extras
- Salt for plating

Steps:
1. Wrap the oysters in greaseproof paper to prevent tearing when sealed in the vacuum.
2. Once wrapped vacuum seal at 100%.
3. Cook at 62C for 30 mins.
4. Drop in an ice bath.
5. Finely chop the lemon grass.
6. Vacuum with the oil and cook at 60C for 8 hours.
7. Strain and store in a squeezy bottle.
8. Spin the strawberry puree in the centrifuge for 2 hours.
9. Separate solid and put to one side.
10. Use 200g of the liquid mix with the lemon juice and the orange blossom.
11. Store in a squeezy bottle.
12. Mix the salt and water.
13. Dissolve and store in two containers with ice.

Other details:

name: Razor Clam, Tamarind
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_3814_parsed
Name: Razor Clam, Tamarind

Ingredients:
- 1 kg large razor clams
- 120 g tamarind paste
- 100 g fish sauce
- 100 g fish syrup
- 10 g sriracha
- 100 g sake
- 100 g dashi
- 100 g hops oil
- Pineapple sage leaves

Steps:
1. Place the clams in cold water to clean them.
2. Re-circulate the water for 1 hour.
3. Once they are clean, remove them from the water.
4. Categorize them by size and place in vacuum bag, facing the same direction. Vacuum on 90%.
5. Cook in boiling water for 45 seconds then stop heating.
6. Once cooked and cooled clean, remove from shell and cut the long mussel part of the razor clam.
7. Trim and bag and freeze for sauce.
8. Portion the clam, portioning 30 g.
9. Mix the ingredients for the sauce and bring to the boil. Cook for 1 minute.
10. Pass through muslin cloth.
11. Finish with 0.25 g xanthan for every 100 g sauce.

Other details:
extras: Pineapple sage leaves

name: Dou Hua
text:
Recipe ID: Dou Hua_Sheet1_0_parsed
Name: Dou Hua

Ingredients:
- 100g Soy bean
- 1000ml Filter Water
- 1L Soy Milk
- 5g Gypsum powder
- 5g Corn Flour
- 40ml still water

Steps:
1. Soak soy bean with water overnight (12hrs)
2. Drain next day, 100g soy bean (before soak) + 500ml filtered water
3. Blitz and squeeze the soy beans
4. Bring to boil, pass through muslin cloth. Cool down and keep it for the next day
5. Bring the soy milk to boiled then pass through muslin again
6. Cool the soy milk down to 82C (while waiting mix the gypsum powder and corn flour and still water in a bowl)
7. Mix the soy milk and the gypsum solution together and cover it with cling film. Let it set for 30min at room temperature.

Other details:
total_time: 48 min
notes: ['Can keep soy milk up to 4 days', 'Have soy milk ready, but make dou hua daily for freshness']

name: Reproducción y cuidado de madres de Kombucha
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_2094_parsed
Name: Reproducción y cuidado de madres de Kombucha

Ingredients:


Steps:
1. La Kombucha es una bebida fermentada de ligero sabor acido que se obtiene con el cuidado de una madre que fermenta desde una generacion anterior.
2. Esta colonia de microorganismos se conoce como SCOBY y el acronimo de la colonia simbiotica de bacterias y levaduras (simplyficado: LA MADRE).
3. La madre Kombucha o Scoby esta vivo y por tanto se alimenta, tiene un metabolismo, se reproduce y puede morir. Hay que cuidar de la Kombucha siempre teniendo presente eso.
4. El ciclo de la madre Kombucha es de entre 10-15 dias. Durante ese tiempo la madre consumira azucar y la Kombucha estara lista cuando el pH este entre 2 y 3, siendo el pH optimo el 3-4.
5. Para alimentar la Kombucha hay que retirar la madre y listo para beber, conservando siempre parte del liquido para usarlo como iniciador de la siguiente fermentacion.
6. Al embotellar la Kombucha es ideal que el gas de las levaduras fermentaran el azucar residual, produciendo una bebida seca y naturalmente carbonatada.

Other details:

raw.name: Reproducción y cuidado de madres de Kombucha
steps: La Kombucha es una bebida fermentada de ligero sabor acido que se obtiene con el cuidado de una madre que fermenta desde una generacion anterior.
Esta colonia de microorganismos se conoce como SCOBY y el acronimo de la colonia simbiotica de bacterias y levaduras (simplyficado: LA MADRE).
La madre Kombucha o Scoby esta vivo y por tanto se alimenta, tiene un metabolismo, se reproduce y puede morir. Hay que cuidar de la Kombucha siempre teniendo presente eso.
El ciclo de la madre Kombucha es de entre 10-15 dias. Durante ese tiempo la madre consumira azucar y la Kombucha estara lista cuando el pH este entre 2 y 3, siendo el pH optimo el 3-4.
Para alimentar la Kombucha hay que retirar la madre y listo para beber, conservando siempre parte del liquido para usarlo como iniciador de la siguiente fermentacion.
Al embotellar la Kombucha es ideal que el gas de las levaduras fermentaran el azucar residual, produciendo una bebida seca y naturalmente carbonatada.

name: Caldo ajo negro (Sopa de ajos)
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_2117_parsed
Name: Caldo ajo negro (Sopa de ajos)

Ingredients:
- 150 g cebolla caramelizada
- 2 uni. pimiento rojo
- 370 g pasta de ajo negro
- 350 g champinon
- 350 g seta shiitake
- 150 g acelga
- 100 mL salsa de soja
- 88 g espinaca
- 3 litros de agua
- 100 g pasta de ajo negro

Steps:
1. Mise en Place: Limpiar, pelar y cortar la verdura en mirepoix.
2. Desarrollo: En una olla amplia, caramelizar la cebolla.
3. Saltear los pimientos, los champinones, las setas shiitake y la acelga.
4. Verter todas las verduras en un olla y anadir la pasta de ajo negro y la salsa de soja. Mezclar.
5. Cocinar el conjunto durante 6 horas a fuego medio y mover cada 15 minutos.
6. Pasado el tiempo, colar por superbag. El caldo pesara 660 ml. aprox.
7. Anadir 100 g de pasta de ajo negro y mezclar.
8. Colar si necesario y porcionar el caldo en bolsas con 100 ml. cada una y reservar en congelacion.

Other details:
notes: Mugaritz 2017

name: Sea Urchin and Coffee
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_3808_parsed
Name: Sea Urchin and Coffee

Ingredients:
- 1kg sea urchin
- 350g Mushroom stock
- 25g Espresso
- 375g Cream
- 4 eggs
- Lemon

Steps:
1. Using a pair of open the urchin from the bottom in a circular direction.
2. Clean in water and remove the urchin tongues into water to rinse them.
3. Remove the tongues and store in container on grease proof paper.
4. Mix all the ingredients together in cold.
5. Fill each ramekin with 30g of mix and place in gastro.
6. Clingfilm the gastro and steam at 93C for 12 minutes until just set.
7. Allow to cool in the tray the store in the fridge.
8. Keep for no more that 2 days.
9. In service place in the oven at 64C for 10 minutes.
10. Once warm place 3 tongues of urchin on top of the custard finish with lemon zest.

Other details:

name: Pastelillo de arroz
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_2075_parsed
Name: Pastelillo de arroz

Ingredients:
- 300 g arroz Yume Nishiki
- 270 ml agua
- 2 g koji

Steps:
1. Desinfectar las bandejas que se utilizaran para la elaboracion con alcohol y papel.
2. Dejar el arroz en remojo durante 30 minutos.
3. Servir el arroz en el cocedor de arroz automatico anadiendo el agua correspondiente.
4. Cocer durante 37 minutos en programa 'White Rice'.
5. Remover la masa de quesitos y servir en la bandeja.
6. Tapar con film y hacer agujeros a este film.
7. Retirar el film y dejar respirar 1 minuto.
8. Jugar este arroz y cambiar su color.
9. Pasado el tiempo, dejar enfriar a 30°C-35°C durante 12 horas.
10. Cortar de esta 10 raciones exactas.

Other details:

1. Vac the two together and cook at 60C for 8 hours.
2. Pass and reserve in the fridge.
3. Blitz Parmesan to a fine crumb.
4. Bring the milk to the boil and pour over the cheese.
5. Add the xanthan and the oil; allow to blend with cold egg whites.
6. Once cooled add gelatin and pass.
7. Reserve in the fridge.
8. Serve very cold.
9. Carefully remove the fillets from the bones.
10. Vac the bones at 90C and transfer to a pan.
11. Scrape clean the pan.
12. Store in an airtight container.
13. Pick the biggest leaves and toast on the yakitori.
14. Once toasted dry at 55C for 8 hours.
15. Store in airtight container.

Other details:

name: Grape, Bee Pollen and Yogurt
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_3788_parsed
Name: Grape, Bee Pollen and Yogurt

Ingredients:
- 200g Grapes
- 5g star anise
- 100g sugar
- 500g Yogurt
- 150g Cream 35%
- 15g Gelatin
- 50g Bee pollen powder

Steps:
1. Peel the grapes and refrigerate.
2. Portion at 20g.
3. Blend sugar with spice.
4. Hydrate the gelatin in ice water.
5. Once hydrated, dissolve in the cream.
6. Mix in the yogurt and pass, no charges.
7. Mix together and store in airtight container.
8. Using a silpat, spread silpat with the mixture.
9. Cook at 90°C.
10. Cook until the caramel melts.
11. Once melted, turn onto another silpat.
12. Heat again in microwave and pull to make shards.
13. Store in air tight container with silica gel.

Other details:

name: Rabbit and Pork Dumplings
text:
Recipe ID: Rabbit And Pork Dumplings_Sheet1_0_parsed
Name: Rabbit and Pork Dumplings

Ingredients:
- 500 gr Pork shoulder
- 250 gr Pork back fat
- 500 gr Rabbit farmed
- 4 Dried Shitake (rehydrated)
- 50 gr Celery
- 20 gr Coriander stalks
- 20 g Salt
- 40 Soy
- 20 Sugar
- 75 Sesame Paste
- 45 Onion Fat
- 50 Corn starch

Steps:
1. To make onion fat, render the pork fat first, then cook sliced onion and garlic together on medium heat until onion gets golden color. Strain and keep it cool.
2. Break the rabbit and take meat off from the bone. Try not to waste any part of the meat and remove all meat as much as you can. Cut all the meat into 5mm cubes in the same manner.
3. Pork meat and Pork Fat need to be cut in the same manner.
4. Place all meat in a mixing bowl and add seasoning, mix well for 30 seconds.
5. Cut rehydrated shitake, celery, coriander stalks into small brunoise and add into meat mix. Keep refrigerated.
6. Make the Beijing boiled style dumpling dough and make them into dumplings (Approx 8gr of dough, 20gr meat).
7. To boil dumplings, cook them for 7 minutes in slightly salty water.
8. While you are boiling the dumplings, prepare the Jade Soup. This green soup has a silky texture with corn flour as some Chinese soups have and it should be fresh and delicious by just drinking itself.
9. Soup must be done portion by portion each time to order. You cannot prepare a batch before service.
10. Also, you must have very fine fresh master stock.
11. First, grate broccoli head on microplane, cut parsley very fine, carrot and celery into fine brunoise and keep separate.
12. Heat up 200ml and 2tbs of white soy in the saucepan and to finish silky look you add 2tbs of cornstarch water.
13. Add broccoli tops 3tbs, celery stalks 1.5 tbs, carrot top 1.5 tbs, cook for 1 minute and take off the heat.
14. When the dumplings get cooked, place them in a bowl, reheat soup, add finely 3tbs chopped parsley and a pinch of white pepper.
15. Mix well and pour over the dumplings. Serve very hot.

Other details:
total_time: 1 hour
notes: ['Do not mince meat with knife, cut must be clean and even with sharp knife.', 'You can use meat mix for a maximum of 3 days, so you need to stay on top of your orderings and preps.']

raw.name: Rabbit and Pork Dumplings
steps: To make onion fat, render the pork fat first, then cook sliced onion and garlic together on medium heat until onion gets golden color. Strain and keep it cool.
Break the rabbit and take meat off from the bone. Try not to waste any part of the meat and remove all meat as much as you can. Cut all the meat into 5mm cubes in the same manner.
Pork meat and Pork Fat need to be cut in the same manner.
Place all meat in a mixing bowl and add seasoning, mix well for 30 seconds.
Cut rehydrated shitake, celery, coriander stalks into small brunoise and add into meat mix. Keep refrigerated.
Make the Beijing boiled style dumpling dough and make them into dumplings (Approx 8gr of dough, 20gr meat).
To boil dumplings, cook them for 7 minutes in slightly salty water.

While you are boiling the dumplings, prepare the Jade Soup. This green soup has a silky texture with corn flour as some Chinese soups have and it should be fresh and delicious by just drinking itself.
Soup must be done portion by portion each time to order. You cannot prepare a batch before service.
Also, you must have very fine fresh master stock.
First, grate broccoli head on microplane, cut parsley very fine, carrot and celery into fine brunoise and keep separate.
Heat up 200ml and 2tbs of white soy in the saucepan and to finish silky look you add 2tbs of cornstarch water.
Add broccoli tops 3tbs, celery stalks 1.5 tbs, carrot top 1.5 tbs, cook for 1 minute and take off the heat.
When the dumplings get cooked, place them in a bowl, reheat soup, add finely 3tbs chopped parsley and a pinch of white pepper.
Mix well and pour over the dumplings. Serve very hot.

notes: Do not mince meat with knife, cut must be clean and even with sharp knife.
You can use meat mix for a maximum of 3 days, so you need to stay on top of your orderings and preps.

name: Creme brulee di zucca
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_5715_parsed
Name: Creme brulee di zucca

Ingredients:
- 1 kg di zucca pezzi
- 15 gr di sale bilanciato x verdura
- Olio
- Alloro
- 400 gr di zucca cotta (elaborazione precedente)
- 150 gr di panna
- 2 uova

Steps:
1. Salare i pezzi di zucca e metterli in un sacchetto sottovuoto da cottura con un goccio d'olio e 2 foglie di alloro.
2. Cuocere a 82° vapore per circa 30 min. raffreddare.
3. Mescolare tutti gli ingredienti e frullare.
4. Passare al chinoise e mettere negli stampi.
5. Cuocere a 92° vapore con pellicola, il tempo dipende dalla grandezza degli stampi.
6. Stampo fan mignon. 15 min.

name: Sichuan Chili Oil
text:
Recipe ID: Sichuan Chili Oil_parsed
Name: Sichuan Chili Oil

Ingredients:
- 3000g (3L) Rapeseed Oil
- 180g Garlic
- 200g Ginger
- 30g Star Anise
- 18g Toasted Coriander Seed
- 600g Whole Dried Red Chili
- 150g Red Sichuan Pepper Corn
- 44g Salt
- 20g Sesame seeds

Steps:
1. Toast Dried chili, Sichuan peppercorn and blitz.
2. In a pot on low heat combine oil with bruised garlic, bruised ginger and toasted coriander seeds.
3. Confit until garlic and ginger is golden brown.
4. Remove from heat and strain, discard garlic, ginger, coriander seeds.
5. Heat oil to 100-120C and add blitzed chilis.
6. Let steep for minimum 2 days.

Other details:

name: Fish Sauce
text:
Recipe ID: fish sauce_Sheet1_0_parsed
Name: Fish Sauce

Ingredients:
- 15 kg Fish Bones
- 100 gr Ginger
- 500 gr Spring Onion
- 50 gr Garlic
- 8500 gr Fish Stock
- 900 gr Ginger peeled sliced
- 600 gr Premium soy
- 30 gr Fish sauce
- 200 gr Black Vinegar
- 100 gr Sugar

Steps:
1. Bring Stock to the boil, add ginger and slow down the fire. Infuse for 15 minutes, add the rest of the liquid. Simmer for 5 more minutes.
2. Clean Fish Bones in cold running water.
3. Remove all guts and eyes.
4. Rinse until no blood is left on the bones.
5. Place bones in a large pan covered with water and bring to the boil. Once boiling, keep skimming.
6. Add all vegetables and keep simmering for 3 hours.
7. After stock is ready, cool down and pass through muslin cloth.
8. Vacuum in small 500 gr portions and keep in freezer.
9. Make sure before you vacuum sauce it's 100% cooled down and cold.

Other details:

name: Fermented Kohlrabi
text:
Recipe ID: Fermented Kohlrabi_parsed
Name: Fermented Kohlrabi

Ingredients:
- 1kg Kohlrabi; Peeled (no green, sliced)
- 20g Maldon Salt
- 5g Sugar
- 30g Ginger
- 10g Garlic
- 1pc chili

Steps:
1. Weigh kohlrabi and for each 1 kg kohlrabi season with 2% Maldon Salt, 0.5% sugar, 30g piece of peeled sliced ginger, 10g garlic, 1 pc of seedless long chili.
2. Check variety of chili with senior team.
3. Peel the external green color of kohlrabi and slice kohlrabi using a mandoline to approximately 2-3 mm thick slices.
4. Check with a senior team on thickness.
5. Wearing gloves, combine everything together.

name: Masa madre de arroz
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_2102_parsed
Name: Masa madre de arroz

Ingredients:
- 750 g harina de arroz
- 250 g agua
- 100 ml agua

Steps:
1. En un bol, mezclar 750 g de harina de arroz y 250 g de agua hasta conseguir una masa firme.
2. Mezclar esta masa con el arroz y dejar fermentar en un bote de cristal limpio.
3. La fermentacion actua como un 'starter' del ciclo de fermentacion que comienza a temperatura ambiente de 48 horas aprox.
4. Si la masa genera gas, espolvorear con burbujas, estara preparada para la elaboracion del plato.
5. Conservar la masa guardada, seguir alimentando el arroz en proporcion de 1:1:1.
6. Si la masa no genera gas, dejarla fermentar 24 horas mas.

Other details:
notes: Es muy importante que cuando generemos cantidad de masa, alimentar cada masa individualmente.

name: Oysters and Tiger Pearls
text:
Recipe ID: Oysters and Tiger Pearls_parsed
Name: Oysters and Tiger Pearls

Ingredients:
- 150 g Tapioca Pearls
- 20 g Dark Soy
- 500 g Water
- 200 g Tiger Dressing
- 1 Oyster
- 10 g Tiger Pearls
- 0.01 g Fine Chives for garnish
- 0.1 g Kow Choi Oil

Steps:
1. Bring together water and dark soy to a boil and whisk in Tapioca Pearls.
2. Cook for 15-20 minutes whisking every so often to prevent tapioca from sticking to the bottom.
3. Strain through a sieve and run quickly under cold water to stop cooking and wash excess starch.
4. Place in container and add Tiger Dressing to absorb.
5. Steam oysters at 100C for 3-4 minutes.
6. Place immediately in Blast Freezer to stop cooking.
7. Shuck open oyster.

Other details:

name: Mole Mancho
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_3781_parsed
Name: Mole Mancho

Ingredients:
- 75g White onion
- 28g Garlic
- 750g Red tomato fresh
- 75g Ancho
- 200g Cascabel
- 100g Pasilla
- 500g Panocha
- 500g Piloncillo
- 2g Oregano
- 5g Cinnamon bark
- 2 litre Chicken stock

Steps:
1. Deseed chilis and open out flat.
2. Toast on a hotplate or open flame (just charring the skin).
3. Rehydrate in water (preferably overnight soak).
4. Sweat down onion and garlic.
5. Toast spices.
6. Place all ingredients in the oven at 200 degrees for 20 minutes (90% humidity if possible).
7. Blend all together, thermomix.
8. Cool in refrigerator and spin three times (makes two paces).

Other details:
notes: Extras

name: Tocino de ternera asado "al hueso"
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_1504_parsed
Name: Tocino de ternera asado "al hueso"

Ingredients:
- Costillas de vaca
- 2 kg grasa de txuleta
- 400 g tendon de aquiles de vaca
- 1 g goma Xantana

Steps:
1. Limpiar toda la carne de los huesos.
2. Cortar los huesos en trozos de 15 centimetros.
3. Blanquear los huesos en agua hirviendo con una espumadera.
4. Secar y rociar con vinagre.
5. Reservar.
6. Cortar la grasa en dados de 5 cm aprox.
7. Cocinar a 90°C durante 20 min.
8. Revolver cada 3 minutos para que la grasa quede limpia.
9. Reservar.
10. Sellar tendones en bolsa de vacio de coccion y cocinar a 90°C durante 12 horas.
11. Colocar en bandeja con rejilla para enfriar.
12. Lijar los tendones separando la carne del tendon.
13. Colocar los tendones en procesador de alimentos.
14. Agregar goma Xantana y mezclar.
15. Colar por colador fino.
16. Reservar en camara.

Other details:
servings: Para 4 personas

name: Mushroom Rice
text:
Recipe ID: Mushroom Rice_parsed
Name: Mushroom Rice

Ingredients:
- 1 bottle Sesame Oil
- 50g Fresh Ginger
- 400g Rehydrated sliced shitake mushroom
- Superior light soy to taste
- Ginger Infused Sesame Oil
- 1300g Glutinous Rice
- 1200g Dashi
- 400g Mushroom for Rice
- 75g Japanese White Soy
- 30g Shaoxing Wine
- 5g Ground White Pepper
- 10g Maldon Salt

Steps:
1. Heat pan with a small amount of rapeseed oil to fry mushroom until golden.
2. Add superior light soy to soak into mushrooms.
3. Combine ingredients together.
4. Wrap securely with cling film and steam at 100C for 20-25 minutes.
5. Stir, taste and check with senior member. Adjust with more dashi if needed.
6. Rewrap and steam for another 15-20 minutes.
7. Check and taste again.

Other details:
portion_size: 180g

name: Yogurt, Granola and Carrot Jam
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_3801_parsed
Name: Yogurt, Granola and Carrot Jam

Ingredients:
- 500g Peeled plums
- 500g Sugar
- 20g Pine branches
- 500g of carrot jam
- 500g Sugar
- 500g Rolled oats
- 200g Rice chips
- 200g Coconut flour
- 200g Black pepper sugar
- 100g Orange zest
- 25g Milk skim
- 20g Fresh ginger
- 50g Plum syrup

Steps:
1. Vac the ingredients together at 100% then leave in the fridge for 4 days until the sugar has dissolved.
2. Once sugar has dissolved pass and store the syrup.
3. Keep the fruit as well.
4. Vac the ingredients together at 100% then leave in the fridge for 4 days until the sugar has dissolved.
5. Remove and store in fridge.
6. Over cook rice in water.
7. Blend and dehydrate.
8. Spread at 180 until crisp.
9. Fry at 180 until crisp.
10. Over cook rice.
11. Blend with hand blender.
12. Spread and dehydrate.
13. Spread at 180 until crisp.
14. Fry at 180 until crisp.
15. Mix all the ingredients together in a large bowl.
16. Spread out on a tray and over bake at 150C.
17. Allow to cool and store in airtight container.

Other details:

name: Grilled Fish Vinegar
text:
Recipe ID: Grilled Fish Vinegar_Sheet1_0_parsed
Name: Grilled Fish Vinegar

Ingredients:
- 1000 g Rice wine Vinegar
- 200 g Caster Sugar
- 20 g Table Salt
- 200 g Fish Bones, Grilled
- 2 g Smoke powder

Steps:
1. Roast the fish bones in the rational oven at 180°C for 30 mins.
2. Whisk the salt and sugar into the rice vinegar.
3. Add all ingredients together and store in a plastic container.
4. Keep out to infuse at room temperature for at least 1 month.

Other details:
notes: Takes a month to infuse!

name: Ventresca de bonito/garum/halofilas
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_2081_parsed
Name: Ventresca de bonito/garum/halofilas

Ingredients:
- 1 kg anchoas frescas
- 20% de sal
- Hinojo
- Romero
- Albahaca
- Perejil
- 1 ventresca de bonito

Steps:
1. Limpiar el contenedor donde se va a hacer el garum.
2. Introducir en el contenedor algunas anchoas con el objeto de facilitar el acceso de las enzimas que realizaran el proceso.
3. Hacer capas de anchoas, sal y hierbas hasta llenar el contenedor.
4. Tapar bien y fermentar 10 dias a 40°.
5. Colar por un filtro fino y almacenar en refrigeracion.
6. La ventresca: Limpiar la ventresca de todo tipo de impurezas, sangre y las espinas.
7. Quitar la membrana que cubre las visceras.
8. Retirar la piel y espinas y colocar en la mezcla de sal y azucar.
9. Mantener 6 h y limpiar.
10. Deshojar y retirar la parte de clostridium botulinum.
11. Cortar y cuadrar.
12. Sobre una bandeja, untar con el garum el lomo de ventresca y anadir las halofilas.

Other details:
notes: El material para hacer el garum conviene que este impregnado de sal.
additional_info: El 20% de sal en el garum puede hacerse en este tipo de material.
source: Mugaritz 2017

name: Dry Pineapple Slice
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_5791_parsed
Name: Dry Pineapple Slice

Ingredients:
- Small Victoria Pineapple: 1
- Water: 1.25kg
- Stock Syrup: 250gr

Steps:
1. Slice the pineapple with the skin with the ham-slicer at 1.5mm.
2. Dice the slices in the syrup.
3. Dehydrate the slices.
4. Remove all the bits from on the slices and put them on a cling filmed and greased dehydrator tray.
5. Dry for 4 hours at 50°C then flip the slices and place a plastic dehydrator sheet on top and dehydrate till fully dry.
6. Store flat in a plastic box with silica-gel. (Label 5 days)

Other details:

name: Layers of Mushroom
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_3765_parsed
Name: Layers of Mushroom

Ingredients:
- For the Ganache
- 300g Double cream
- 200g White chocolate
- 200g Freeze-dried mushroom powder (sieved)
- Salt
- For the mushroom stock
- 200g Dried shiitake mushrooms
- 200g Button mushrooms
- 200g Brown mushrooms
- 200g Black trumpet
- 41g Water
- For the mushroom jelly
- 200g Mushroom stock
- 1.2g Agar agar
- For the syrup
- 500g Water
- 500g Sugar
- 100g Trimoline
- 10g Liquid glucose

Steps:
1. Bring the cream to the boil and pour over the chocolate. Leave for 1 minute and add the mushroom powder, stick blend.
2. Pour into a paco jet container and cool in the fridge.
3. Once chilled, pass in the paco jet twice until well incorporated.
4. Transfer to a piping bag.
5. Add all ingredients in stockpot, bring to the boil, pass the stock and keep the mushrooms.
6. Dissolve the agar in the mushroom stock, bring to the boil while whisking constantly and weigh 500g.
7. Once set, cut into rectangles of 6cm x 3cm.
8. Reserve in container in fridge.

Other details:

name: Suckling Pork
text:
Recipe ID: Suckling Pork_Sheet1_0_parsed
Name: Suckling Pork

Ingredients:
- Ginger
- Garlic
- Sichuan salt
- Peanut oil
- Butcher twins
- Rice vinegar
- Chinese red vinegar
- Lemon juice
- Kao Liang Wine

Steps:
1. Wash, clean inside out. Split suckling pig in half. Blow torch all hair away.
2. Place pig over the sink on the rack and pour piping hot water all over the skin.
3. Pat pork dry from inside out, slit the knees and tie back with butcher strings.
4. Spice rub: Garlic 5 cloves, Ginger 1/5 knob roughly chopped both and Sichuan salt.
5. Use spice mix, Sichuan salt rub outside the pork and inside, inside the pork only use ginger and garlic rub.
6. After marinated, place pork flat skin side up and leave to marinate in walking fridge for 4 hours.
7. Once marinated, wipe the skin side only with jaycloth and brush with vinegar liquor.
8. Apply evenly everywhere on the skin and do 2 times. After, place skin up on gastro tray and leave in blast chiller until skin will be dry and absorbed all mixture.
9. After service is over, make sure your josper stays hot at 150 C. Brush off all spices, ginger and garlic off.
10. Place pig skin up in shallow 1/1 gastro tray in josper at 160 C with door closed.
11. Next morning, take pork out of the josper and place in rational oven at 290 C without head.
12. Make sure you cook pork until skin gets nice brown color and crispy.
13. Take pig from rational time to time to brush with peanut oil.
14. Head: the ears and nose need to be covered by foil.

Other details:

name: Tomates
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_2084_parsed
Name: Tomates

Ingredients:
- El tomate fermentado
- Tomates maduros
- Sal

Steps:
1. Lavar los tomates, eliminar las partes podridas que pueda tener.
2. Esguarzar con las manos en un gastronorm alto.
3. La fermentacion empezara al cabo de unas horas, espumeara y se hara alcoholica.
4. El gas carbonico se desprendera y las burbujas de aire se amplian.
5. Remover ocasionalmente el metodo con el tomate en fermentacion.
6. Despues de 4 o 5 dias, dependiendo de la temperatura, el tomate estara completamente fermentado.
7. Recuperar la pasta de tomate del interior y prensar.
8. Servir sobre un tamiz fino para que pierda el liquido y se obtenga una masa firme.

Other details:
annotation: El tomate fermentado ha sido durante cientos de anos un manjar. Se preparan en la epoca con mas produccion de tomate.
source: The art of fermentation, Sandor Ellix Katz, Pag. 117

Servir sobre un tamiz fino para que pierda el liquido y se obtenga una masa firme.

other_details.annotation: El tomate fermentado ha sido durante cientos de anos un manjar. Se preparan en la epoca con mas produccion de tomate.
other_details.source: The art of fermentation, Sandor Ellix Katz, Pag. 117

name: Peanut Brittle and Chocolate and Galabé Sugar Crèmeux
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_5794_parsed
Name: Peanut Brittle and Chocolate and Galabé Sugar Crèmeux

Ingredients:
- Water: 25gr
- Glucose: 25gr
- Caster sugar: 40gr
- Chopped peanuts: 120gr
- White sugar: 120gr
- Butter, melted: 40gr
- Egg: 1
- Whipping cream: 150gr
- Milk: 150gr
- Galabé sugar: 50gr
- Egg yolks: 60gr
- Caster sugar: 15gr
- Fresh pastry custard: 300gr
- Dark chocolate: 125gr
- Gelatine leaves: 8gr

Steps:
1. Mix and dissolve the water, glucose and caster sugar. Heat to 150°C.
2. Mix in the chopped peanuts and pour onto baking parchment. Place another sheet of parchment over the top and roll out as thinly as possible. Leave to cool.
3. Finely chop the hardened, cold brittle.
4. Combine the white sugar and butter, mix until fluffy.
5. Add the egg and mix thoroughly.
6. Sift the flour and baking powder, then add to the mixture (above).
7. Sprinkle the dough out onto a baking sheet and bake at 160°C for 15 minutes. Break into pieces.
8. Bag and keep in the dry store. (label 1 month)
9. Pour the cream to 90°C and add sugar and pectine then cook to 90°C.
10. Pour a third of the milk over the yolks.
11. Return everything to the pan.
12. Pass.
13. Combine the custard with the chocolate and the soaked gelatine and blend with hand blender.
14. Pass and pour into a plastic container.
15. Wrap and keep in the fridge for 1 day before use. (label 5 days)

Other details:
temperature: 150°C, 160°C, 90°C
storage: Bag and keep in the dry store. (label 1 month), Wrap and keep in the fridge for 1 day before use. (label 5 days)

name: Viili
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_2079_parsed
Name: Viili

Ingredients:
- 1 L leche liquida
- 1 cucharada leche en polvo
- 25 g masa madre

Steps:
1. Calentar la leche hasta que alcance los 90°C.
2. Enfriar a temperatura ambiente.
3. Mezclar en un bote previamente desinfectado y tapado con la masa madre.
4. Conservar el bote a 20°C.
5. Una vez pasado el tiempo y la textura del viili se compacte, refrigerar durante 6 horas antes de usar.

Other details:
notes: Para el exito de la receta, limpiar bien las superficies e instrumentos empleados. Se trabaja con un microorganismo vivo, el viili, por lo que es crucial mantener la higiene.

name: Mackerel and Tomato
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_3804_parsed
Name: Mackerel and Tomato

Ingredients:
- 4 Mackerel
- 4 Tomatoes
- Salt
- Pepper
- Olive oil
- Ricotta cheese

Steps:
1. Clean mackerel and remove fillets.
2. Leave covered in salt for 20 minutes. Rinse and dry.
3. Using tweezers, remove the bones and season fillets with black pepper.
4. Smoke fillets for 15 minutes.
5. Remove skin from the tomatoes and store in fridge skin side up.
6. Once cooked, remove and cook in the oven at 250C for 10 minutes.
7. Remove and allow to cool.
8. Remove fillets and cook in the oven at 250C for 6 minutes.
9. Remove and store in fridge.

Other details:
finishing_and_plating: ['Warm fillets in the holding oven.', 'Place fillets of the mackerel on the eye gazpacho.', 'Top with tomato and spoon of ricotta cheese.']

Top with tomato and spoon of ricotta cheese.

name: Presa and Salted Carrots
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_3779_parsed
Name: Presa and Salted Carrots

Ingredients:
- 1kg Sandy Carrots (mashed)
- 200g Carrot Juice
- 5% Salt
- Kuzu 3.5%
- 2l Grapeseed
- 100g Grand fir

Steps:
1. Rinse well in the sink before peeling.
2. Peel carrots to remove sand remnants.
3. Re-wash again to ensure no sand remains.
4. Slice the carrots and pass through a mandolin to 3mm thickness.
5. Weigh carrots. Add 2% of the total weight in salt and juice.
6. Vacuum and leave at room temperature for 4-7 days.
7. Once fermented, remove carrots and slice to desired thickness.
8. Vacuum slice in bags needed per service and freeze.
9. When needed, add 3.5% kuzu and cook slowly until above 65 degrees and the sauce appears.
10. Defrost Presa the night before.
11. Portion Presa at 140g per portion.
12. Set oil bath to 60 degrees in circulator.
13. Place Presa in oil bath for pasteurisation.
14. Place oil in bath until temperature is 53 degrees.
15. Place on yakitori and smoke over pine.
16. Rest and carve.

Other details:

name: Cristales de tierra con queso de un dia
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_1501_parsed
Name: Cristales de tierra con queso de un dia

Ingredients:
- 5 unid. de remolacha
- 5 unid. de remolacha amarilla
- 5 unid. de remolacha roja
- 50 gr. de azucar
- 250 ml. de vinagre de manzana
- 250 ml. de agua
- 250 gr. de queso fresco
- 30 gr. de leche de vaca
- 3.5 hoja de gelatina

Steps:
1. El cristal de remolacha: Con ayuda de un grosor de 0.5 mm, laminar las remolachas. Envasar al vacio con el azucar y el vinagre de manzana. Cocer a 85°C durante 1 hora. Enfriar hasta que se quede plano.
2. La espuma de queso: Infusionar la leche y el queso fresco. Hidratar la hoja de gelatina en agua con hielo. Por cada 700 ml. de leche infusionada agregar 3.5 hojas de gelatina. Triturar y colar. Envasar en un sifon con 2 cargas de nitrogeno.
3. El acabado: Romper con las manos el cristal del tamano de la vajilla y reservar. Montar la espuma de queso, cubrir con el cristal de remolacha.

Other details:
servings: Para 15 personas
notes: Mugaritz 2017

name: Vegetarian Spring Roll - Tofu Filling
text:
Recipe ID: Vegetarian Spring Roll (1)_Tofu Filling_1_parsed
Name: Vegetarian Spring Roll - Tofu Filling

Ingredients:
- 150g Pressed Tofu
- 85g Chopped Mustard Green
- 85g Wilted Spinach
- 10g Superior Soy
- 5g Aged Soy
- 12g Sesame Oil
- 1g White Pepper
- 1g Maldon Salt

Steps:
1. Press Tofu (Clean Bean) for two hours you should have a loss of 20% of the Weight.
2. Chop and Fry the Mustard greens for a bit, just to lose some of the water content, you should have a 30% loss of the weight at the end.
3. Wash the Spinach, dry it and wilt it in a pan so that it's cooked and the water of spinach has sort of evaporated.
4. Squeeze all the water out of the spinach with a J-cloth.
5. Chop the pressed tofu, Chop the Spinach, mix them together and add the seasonings.
6. Add the chopped preserved mustard to the mix. Taste it and add salt if needed.

Other details:

total_batch_weight: 349g
total_portion: 17.45 portions
portion_size: 20g

name: Manhatten Pigs Tails
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_3784_parsed
Name: Manhatten Pigs Tails

Ingredients:
- 10kg Iberico tails
- Disposable razors

Steps:
1. Cut the safe guard off the top of the razors to expose the blade.
2. Shave the hair off the tails and clean.
3. Place an even layer of tails in each vacuum bag and seal fully.
4. Cook for 6 hours or overnight at 92 degrees on full steam.
5. Remove from the vacuum bags the following day and drain the excess liquid and flesh to use later.
6. Gently, lightly go down the tail.
7. Debone keeping the meat on the skin.
8. Wrap bones tightly in greaseproof paper and vacuum once sealed.
9. Place pig's tail between greaseproof paper in a big pan.
10. Put a weight on top to press the tail flat against the pan.
11. Cook on a flat top until crispy.
12. Trim, season and serve.

Other details:
To prepare: Take from the lab in reduced milk.
To cook: Vacuum and store in reduced milk. For service defrost, full bag per service. Add 45% Kuzu. Dissolve and heat slowly until above 65 degrees and translucent. Season and keep above 65 degrees in the oven or circulator.

name: Kombucha de licuados
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_2091_parsed
Name: Kombucha de licuados

Ingredients:
- 1 l. licuado de fresa
- 90 g. de azucar
- 1 trozo de madre de kombucha

Steps:
1. Llevar la mezcla a ebullicion.
2. Anadir el azucar, el trozo de madre de kombucha y una parte del licuado de fresa.
3. Dejar fermentar a temperatura ambiente.
4. Pasado el tiempo, probar el liquido fermentado y llevar a su punto de fermentacion, embotellar y dejar en nevera una semana antes de usar.

Other details:

name: Cuttlefish Mix
text:
Recipe ID: Cuttlefish Mix 2019_Sheet1_0_parsed
Name: Cuttlefish Mix

Ingredients:
- 2X Bag Frozen Squid
- 2X Bag Frozen Prawns
- 200 G CornFlour
- 40 G Garlic powder
- 100 G Squid ink
- 4 Tsp Sugar
- 2 Tsp White pepper
- 20 ml Rice Vinegar
- 20 ml Sesame Oil
- 4 Egg White

Steps:
1. Defrost the Prawns and Squid.
2. Dice down the squid to help it blend.
3. Blend the prawns and squid down in batches with a little water if needed to help get it smooth.
4. Place a little amount of the puree back into the thermo with the rest of the ingredients apart from the egg whites with a little more puree on top to help blend.
5. Place everything into Kitchen aid mixing bowl and mix until combined.
6. More squid ink may need to be added to get the right colour.

Other details:
notes: Make sure the mix doesn’t get too warm during the blending process.

name: Beetroot with Yuzu
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_3811_parsed
Name: Beetroot with Yuzu

Ingredients:
- 500g flour
- 250g butter
- 200g water
- 150g table salt
- 3 large beetroots
- 1kg salt dough
- 10g ginger
- 40g yuzu juice
- 100g mirin
- 5g soy sauce
- 10g powdered dashi
- 10g lemon juice
- 300g butter
- 120g egg yolk

Steps:
1. Combine all the ingredients together in kenwood.
2. Mix until smooth dough is formed.
3. Store in fridge until needed.
4. Wrap the beets in the dough. The dough should be at least 1cm thick.
5. Bake in the oven for 1 hour at 170C until the beets are cooked.
6. Once cooked, allow to cool then remove from the dough.
7. Break 250g of the dough into pieces roughly.
8. Dry at 60C for 1 hour.
9. Peel and finely chop ginger.
10. Mix all the ingredients together and let steep overnight.
11. Strain and reserve in fridge.
12. Cut butter in to cubes.
13. Mix all ingredients together in a vacuum bag and seal.

Other details:

name: Cucumber, Coconut and Caviar
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_3791_parsed
Name: Cucumber, Coconut and Caviar

Ingredients:
- 1 kg Coconut puree
- 15 leaves gelatine
- 12 g Salt
- 1 kg Horseradish Root
- 500 ml Neutral Oil
- 5 Cucumbers
- 250 g Cucumber Skin Juice
- 50 g Horseradish Vodka
- 300 ml Light Fish Stock
- 200 ml Horseradish/Cucumber
- 30 g Celery
- Xanthan Gum

Steps:
1. Hydrate gelatine in iced water.
2. Warm the coconut puree and melt the gelatine.
3. Add salt and freeze.
4. Once frozen hard, blitz a little at a time in thermos to obtain a fine powder.
5. Peel horseradish and blitz in Thermomix.
6. Add oil and cook in thermo at 90 degrees for 10 minutes.
7. Pass through a chinois into an iced container.
8. Peel cucumbers and keep skins.
9. Use a parisienne scoop to ball the cucumbers.
10. Juice the skins.
11. Mix the juice with the horseradish vodka.
12. Compress cucumber balls in a vac pac machine.
13. Blend fish stock with cucumber and celery.
14. Pass through a coffee filter overnight in fridge.
15. Vac pac to remove air.
16. Bottle and chill.

Other details:

Add salt and freeze.

name: Vermicelli Mix
text:
Recipe ID: Vermicelli Mix_Sheet1_0_parsed
Name: Vermicelli Mix

Ingredients:
- 250 gr Butter
- 50 gr Premium Soy
- 1 gr Gellespressa
- 150 gr Soak vermicelli
- 100 gr Soak black fungus
- 100 gr Soak bamboo fungus
- 150 gr Vermicelli dressing
- 20 gr Chive
- 20 gr Coriander

Steps:
1. Soak mushrooms overnight in cold water. Drain dry and cut into long julienne strips.
2. Place butter and soy into the saucepan.
3. Cook on low fire until butter is melted.
4. Emulsify with soy.
5. Whisk in the gellespressa.
6. Keep it at room temperature.
7. Soak vermicelli overnight, after draining, keep it dry.
8. Mix vermicelli mix on a daily basis depending on how much is being sold.
9. To make mix, mix all together. Service portion is 60 gr.
10. To finish dish, use girolles cooked in emulsion, truffle oil, lemon juice, and black pepper.
11. 5 gr of girolles per portion.

Other details:

name: Tuetano negro
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_1508_parsed
Name: Tuetano negro

Ingredients:
- 2 piezas de piel de cerdo
- 12 g pasta de ajo negro
- 12 g pasta de miso
- 80 g carne de rabito
- 30 g vinagre de sidra
- 20 g guindilla fresca
- 30 ml aceite girasol

Steps:
1. Ensartar las pieles de cerdo y cocinar durante 12 horas a 90°C.
2. Retirar las pieles cocidas, cortarlas y congelarlas.
3. Retirar las pieles del frio, retirar las gelatinas y sacar el exceso de grasa.
4. Cortar la piel de cerdo en tiras finas.
5. Desgrasar la carne reservada de los rabitos.
6. Añadir el ajo negro, miso, carne de rabito y vinagre de sidra.
7. Poner a punto de sal y reservar en manga en refrigeracion.
8. Confitarlas guindillas frescas en el aceite durante 2 horas a 72°C.
9. Pasado el tiempo, tamizar con un colador fino y reservar el aceite.

Other details:
servings: 4 personas

name: Crispy Leaves
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_3770_parsed
Name: Crispy Leaves

Ingredients:
- 100g Dandelion
- 100g Rocket
- 100g Sea Kale
- 100g Seaweed (dried)
- 10g Seaweed Agar Powder
- Olive oil spray
- Vinaigrette

Steps:
1. Pick leaves all the same size (15cm).
2. Wash in cold water 3 times.
3. Bring to boil 12L of water with 50g salt.
4. Blanch leaves for 15 seconds, until bright green, and refresh in iced water.
5. Blend each type of leaf separately.
6. Once fully blended, strain to hold back the leaves correctly, making sure no fiber breaks through.
7. Spread the leaves out flat and not touching on a dehydrator tray.
8. Dry in the dehydrator at 55C for 8 hours.
9. Once dry, remove from the dehydrator and store in an airtight container with silica gel.
10. Blend together to form a fine powder.
11. Store in airtight container.
12. Take out leaves and spray them with the oil.
13. Using a small sieve, dust leaves lightly with the wasabi powder.
14. Arrange the leaves neatly with concrete flower and logan flower inside a serving bowl.
15. Serve on small white plate.

Other details:
temperature: 55C
drying_time: 8 hours

name: Caldo de Rabito
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_2112_parsed
Name: Caldo de Rabito

Ingredients:
- 20 ud. Cabezas de Cochinillo
- 20 l. Agua
- 20 l. Agua

Steps:
1. Dorar las cabezas de cochinillo en el horno a 200°C durante aproximadamente 40 minutos o hasta que adquieran un tono dorado intenso.
2. Introducir las cabezas de cochinillo doradas en una olla de presion. Cubrir con el agua y cerrar bien la olla de presion.
3. Llevar a un fuego intenso hasta que se libere la presion y entonces bajar el fuego al minimo. Mantener asi durante 2 horas y 30 minutos.
4. Enfriar la olla de presion antes de abrirla.
5. Colar el caldo usando un chino. Reservar las cabezas.
6. Colocar el caldo en un recipiente y cubrir con film. Enfriar en el abatidor de temperatura hasta que se solidifique la grasa en la superficie.
7. Retirar la grasa de la superficie.
8. Introducir las cabezas de cochinillo recuperadas del caldo en una olla de presion y volver a cubrir con 20 l. de agua.
9. Cerrar bien la olla de presion y cocinar 2 horas y 30 minutos mas despues de liberar la presion.
10. Colar el caldo usando un chino.
11. Colocar el caldo en un recipiente y cubrir con film. Enfriar en el abatidor de temperatura hasta que se solidifique la grasa en la superficie.
12. Retirar la grasa de la superficie.
13. Añadir el segundo caldo sin grasa a la olla donde se esta realizando la primer reduccion y continuar reduciendo a fuego MUY SUAVE durante 2 horas.
14. Pasar el caldo por un tamiz fino e inmediatamente pasar por un micro colador.
15. Continuar reduciendo hasta obtener 25 brix.
16. Reservar en bolsas de vacio, ya sea en refrigeracion (uso semanal) o en congelacion.

Other details:

name: Civet de sangacho
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_1494_parsed
Name: Civet de sangacho

Ingredients:
- El sangacho de atun: 200 g sangacho
- El acabado: 30 g sangre de liebre (partida de caldos)
- 5 g kuzu
- 12 uni. sangacho

Steps:
1. Desangrar el sangacho de atun. Retirar las espinas. Reservar solo el lomo. Cortar en tamanos regulares y reservar 12 piezas (20 g) en total por racion en refrigeracion.
2. Napar el civet de sangacho en frio, mezclar en el caldo de acabado y disolver el almidon en el liquido de coccion.
3. Asegurarse de disolver el conjunto hasta que no queden grumos.
4. Acercar la mezcla a fuego suave y cocer hasta que espese.
5. Verter el conjunto sobre una bandeja de silicona e introducir en el horno.
6. Asegurarse de que la mezcla se extienda de manera uniforme y que el almidon se disuelva por completo.
7. Retirar del horno y dejar enfriar.
8. Pasado el tiempo, escurrir el sangacho, llevar el caldo del acabado a 80°C (induccion 9), introducir 1 sangacho y dejar 30 segundos, napar el sangacho completamente con el caldo.
9. Envolverlo sin pintar, disponer el sangacho y napar completamente con el caldo.

Other details:
notes: Mugaritz 2017

name: Mushroom, Pine Nut and Garlic
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_3773_parsed
Name: Mushroom, Pine Nut and Garlic

Ingredients:
- 600g Mushrooms
- 200g Dried Shiitakes
- 200g Dried Porcini
- 20g Dried Kombu
- 4l Water
- 50g Sake
- 50g Cooked Mushroom Stock
- 50g Garlic
- 100g Onion
- 200g Roasted Pine Nuts
- 50g Garlic
- 200g King Oyster Mushrooms
- Wild Garlic Oil
- 3 Conserved Garlic Flowers
- Garlic Capers

Steps:
1. Add all ingredients in stockpot, bring to the boil then simmer to reduce by half.
2. Pass the stock and keep the mushrooms.
3. Reduce the stock further if necessary and season.
4. Set the leftover mushrooms to be freeze-dried and turned into powder.
5. Finely chop the garlic, leek, and onion, then sweat them in a little olive oil without coloring.
6. Add the sake and reduce.
7. Remove from heat and blend to a smooth paste.
8. Season with salt.
9. Using a meat slicer or mandolin, slice the mushroom into 1mm thick slices.
10. Neatly cut the slices into thin strips resembling spaghetti.

Other details:

Finely chop the garlic, leek, and onion, then sweat them in a little olive oil without coloring.
Add the sake and reduce.
Remove from heat and blend to a smooth paste.
Season with salt.
Using a meat slicer or mandolin, slice the mushroom into 1mm thick slices.
Neatly cut the slices into thin strips resembling spaghetti.

name: Sopa de ajos
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_2111_parsed
Name: Sopa de ajos

Ingredients:
- 20 uni. dientes de ajo pelados
- 1 l. agua
- 20 g. cal
- 200 ml. leche
- 80 ml. caldo negro
- 20 trozos de pan sopaco
- c/s. pan sopaco
- c/s. sal fina
- 12 uni. ajos nixtamalizados
- c/s. pan rallado
- c/s. clara de huevo
- 80 ml. caldo negro (partida de caldos)
- Rallador

Steps:
1. Tornear los dientes ajos.
2. En un recipiente con agua, agregar la cal. Con ayuda de una cuchara mezclar bien la solucion calcica.
3. Agregar los ajos a la solucion y mantener su inmersion de 1 hora.
4. Es recomendable remover el conjunto de vez en cuando, pues la cal tiende a reposarse y no actua bien de esta manera.
5. Retirarlos del bano de cal y lavarlos con agua fria.
6. En un cazo con leche, cocerlos a fuego mediano durante 30 minutos o hasta que esten tiernos.
7. Colarlos y reservar en refrigeracion.
8. Rallar xx g. de pan sopaco en el caldo negro.
9. Texturizar con el pan. Es muy importante que el caldo tenga textura de sopa.
10. Empanar los ajos en clara de huevo y con pan rallado. Escurrir el exceso.
11. Calentar un cazo con aceite girasol a 180°C. Freir los ajos en el aceite hasta dorados.
12. Disponerlos encima de una bandeja cubierta con papel para escurrir el exceso de aceite. Poner a punto de sal.
13. Disponer los ajos en la base del plato caliente.
14. Disponer 5 trozos pequenos de pan sopaco por racion.
15. Cubrir el conjunto con el caldo negro (20 ml aprox. por racion).
16. Rallar la superficie con pan sopaco. Servir caliente.

Other details:
servings: Para 4 personas
temperature: 180°C
notes: Mugaritz 2017

name: Egg Rice / Tian Jin Rice
text:
Recipe ID: Tian Jin Rice_Sheet1_0_parsed
Name: Egg Rice / Tian Jin Rice

Ingredients:
- 200g Fermented Mustard Green
- 20g Ginger, Brunoise
- 1200g Leftover Chi Shiang Rice, cold and hard
- Veg Oil
- Salt
- 530g Clarence Court Egg, about 10 eggs
- 15g Premium Soy
- 10g Mirin
- Spring onion for garnish
- Ice Water

Steps:
1. Cut the fermented mustard green to 1cm x 1cm size.
2. Preheat the wok, add in a generous amount of oil.
3. Once the oil is on medium high heat, add in the fermented mustard green, fry off for a bit.
4. Add in the diced/brunoise ginger.
5. Continue to fry it off until it's caramelised a bit on the edges.
6. Add more oil if need be.
7. Add the rice and fry. Make sure you separate every grain of rice.
8. It helps by breaking it off when prepping.
9. Season with salt and taste.
10. Once it's all fried off, keep in containers and keep in hot cupboard.
11. Mix all egg mix ingredients together. Mix well.
12. Keep in a jug, and store in the fridge.
13. Use the small upright holder pan to fry the egg. Use chopsticks when cooking to form velvety texture of the egg.
14. Make sure you drain/dry the spring onion before plating on the rice.

Other details:

Ingredients:
- Jerusalem artichoke
- salt
- crushed garlic
- rough ginger
- honey
- soy sauce
- Chingkiang vinegar

Steps:
1. Soak the Jerusalem Artichoke for 10 minutes, and then wash it thoroughly.
2. Cut the Jerusalem artichoke in half or quarter if big.
3. Mix it in a bowl with a bit of salt, crushed garlic and rough ginger.
4. Drizzle the Jerusalem artichoke with honey to glaze.
5. Roast in the oven at 195C for 15 minutes and above until golden and cooked.
6. When golden, deglaze with soy sauce just to give it some taste and a dash of Chingkiang vinegar.

name: Caldo de Cuellos de Pato
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_2123_parsed
Name: Caldo de Cuellos de Pato

Ingredients:
- 10 kg Cuellos de Pato
- 8 kg Cebolla cortada en juliana
- 15 l Agua

Steps:
1. Trocear los cuellos de pato para facilitar su dorado.
2. Estirar las piezas de pato en bandejas con papel sulfurizado.
3. Asar en el horno a 220°C hasta que el pato esté muy dorado. Aproximadamente 40 min.
4. Pochar la cebolla en suficiente aceite hasta conseguir un caramelizado total, uniforme y muy intenso.
5. Introducir la cebolla y el pato en una olla de presión y cubrir con el agua.
6. Cerrar la olla y llevar a hervor sobre un fuego.
7. Cuando la olla libere presión, bajar el fuego al mínimo y mantener una cocción suave durante 2 horas.
8. Enfriar la olla y colar el caldo usando un chino. Presionar bien la carne para obtener la mayor cantidad de caldo posible.
9. Colocar el caldo en un recipiente y cubrir con film. Enfriar en el abatidor de temperatura hasta que se solidifique la grasa en la superficie.
10. Retirar la grasa por completo y colocar el caldo en una olla alta para reducirlo.
11. Reducir el caldo hasta obtener una concentración de 30° en refractómetro.
12. Pasar por un micro colador, ajustar el punto de sal y reservar en bolsas de vacío de 200 g.

Other details:
servings: Para 1.5 l

id: Tian Jin Rice_Hot and Sour Soup_Sheet1_0_parsed
name: Hot and Sour Soup for Tian Jin Rice
text:
Recipe ID: Tian Jin Rice_Hot and Sour Soup_Sheet1_0_parsed
Name: Hot and Sour Soup for Tian Jin Rice

Ingredients:
- 1 knob Ginger
- 300 ml Dashi
- Sesame Oil
- 50 g Carrot, Finely Sliced
- 25 g Fresh Shiitake Mushroom, Finely Sliced
- Salt
- 1 tbsp White Pepper
- 3 tbsp Premium Soy
- 2 tbsp Rice Wine Vinegar Kong Yen
- 1 tbsp Ching Kiang Vinegar
- 1 tsp Mirin
- Corn Starch Water

Steps:
1. Infuse dashi with crushed ginger.
2. Finely slice the carrot and shiitake. Really fine like toothpicks.
3. In a pan, put in 2-3 tbsp of sesame oil.
4. Once the sesame oil is hot, add the carrots and shiitake.
5. Season with salt and white pepper.
6. Once the carrot and shiitake are nicely sweated, add the dashi without the ginger, discard ginger.
7. Bring up to boil.
8. Season with premium soy, kong yen rice wine vinegar, ching kiang vinegar, mirin. Taste and see if it needs more salt.
9. Take the pan off the heat source. Stir the hot and sour soup in one hand, and pour in the cornstarch water in the other. Keep stirring.
10. Bring back to the heat, stir and adjust thickness to a velvety texture.

Other details:
notes: Taste profile should be very peppery from the white pepper.

name: Halibut, Toasted Rice
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_3812_parsed
Name: Halibut, Toasted Rice

Ingredients:
- 80g Halibut
- 500g Neutral oil
- 100g Lemon thyme
- 200g Grape seed oil
- 2kg Fish bones
- 200g White wine
- 3l Water
- 750g Fish stock
- 325g Rice
- 50g Kombu
- 10g Burnt garlic
- 1g Long pepper

Steps:
1. Warm the oil in the circulator to 62C.
2. Temper the fish and place in the oil.
3. Bring the fish up to 42C.
4. Remove from the oil and torch the skin with a blowtorch.
5. Pick all the leaves off the branches.
6. Blend, squeeze over and add to oil.
7. Blend until smooth and pass through muslin onto ice.
8. Roast the fish bones until golden brown in the oven.
9. Transfer to a large pan and add the white wine and reduce.
10. Once reduced, cover with the water.
11. Cook for 20 minutes then pass.
12. Reduce until desired flavor is reached.
13. Toast the rice at 170C for 9 minutes until golden brown.
14. Warm the stock and add the rest of the ingredients and infuse 15 minutes.

Other details:

raw.name: Halibut, Toasted Rice
steps: Warm the oil in the circulator to 62C.
Temper the fish and place in the oil.
Bring the fish up to 42C.
Remove from the oil and torch the skin with a blowtorch.
Pick all the leaves off the branches.
Blend, squeeze over and add to oil.
Blend until smooth and pass through muslin onto ice.
Roast the fish bones until golden brown in the oven.
Transfer to a large pan and add the white wine and reduce.
Once reduced, cover with the water.
Cook for 20 minutes then pass.
Reduce until desired flavor is reached.
Toast the rice at 170C for 9 minutes until golden brown.
Warm the stock and add the rest of the ingredients and infuse 15 minutes.

name: Cockles with Fermented Chili Dressing
text:
Recipe ID: _Cockles with Fermented Chili Dressing_Introduction_0_parsed
Name: Cockles with Fermented Chili Dressing

Ingredients:
- 23 Cockles
- 200g Fermented Chili Dressing
- 1g Picked Chervil
- 5g Chicken Skin

Steps:
1. Steam the cockles and open them, removing one of the shells.
2. Arrange the cockles in a claypot and cover with a wet cloth until ready to serve.
3. If there is leftover cockles liquid, leave some at the bottom of the claypot.
4. At serving time, drape the cockles with the sauce, put a lid on, and steam until hot.
5. Add picked chervil and crispy chicken skin, put the lid back on, and serve.

Other details:
allergens: Mustard, Egg, Soy
notes: Ideally the presentation, half shell cockles all face up around in a circle, with a drape of sauce on top, picked chervil and chicken skin.

name: Mushroom Dumpling
text:
Recipe ID: Mushroom Dumpling_Sheet1_0_parsed
Name: Mushroom Dumpling

Ingredients:
- 750g Fresh Shiitake
- 500g Oyster Mushroom
- 160g Dried Shiitake (Soak in 600g water to rehydrate)
- 600g Mushroom Water or veggie dashi
- 30g Diced ginger
- 200ml Shaoxin Wine
- 150ml Light Soy
- 400g Lotus root grated (finely grated)
- Oil

Steps:
1. Cut mushrooms to rough 1cm size
2. Blitz the rehydrated shiitake coarsely
3. In a hot pan, fry the diced ginger
4. Once nicely browned, add blitzed rehydrated shiitake
5. Then add chopped fresh shiitake and chopped Oyster mushroom
6. Add and pinch of salt, then further to cook until wilted
7. Add shao xin wine and let it evaporate
8. Add soy sauce and taste mushroom
9. Add grated lotus root and cook to combine
10. Add Mushroom water and let it cook down and form a good consistency

Other details:
chilli_oil_dressing: 1:1= Sichuan chilli oil: Tiger dressing
dumpling_ratio: 10g dough, 20g fillings
each_portion: 1 tbsp chilli oil dressing, 1/2 tsp chilli pulp, 3 Piece dumplings
note: dumpling skin not to flap 水餃皮縮口要窄才會挺
photo_needed: True

name: Kombucha de lemongrass
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_2092_parsed
Name: Kombucha de lemongrass

Ingredients:
- 2.5 l agua
- 30 g lemongrass
- 100 g azucar blanco
- 1/2 taza de kombucha madre

Steps:
1. Abrir el lemongrass cortando la punta a lo largo.
2. Hervir el lemongrass en el agua.
3. Retirar del fuego el agua con lemongrass y dejar infusionar.
4. Colar el liquido.
5. Una vez frio, anadir el azucar.
6. Anadir el trozo de kombucha madre.
7. Dejar fermentar a temperatura ambiente.
8. Pasado el tiempo, probar si el liquido embotellado y dejar en nevera una semana antes de usar.

Other details:
servings: Para 2
notes: Mayo 2017

name: Plum Petite Four
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_3792_parsed
Name: Plum Petite Four

Ingredients:
- Cal X: 10g cal x, 1 litre water
- Plums Powder: 1x per pax
- Plums: 1x per pax
- Sugar: 600g
- Water: 1000ml
- Agar: 10g
- Agar Blossom Sugar: 10g

Steps:
1. Whisk Cal X into measured water.
2. Keep mixing during use.
3. Peel plums with sharp peeler.
4. Retain skins and dehydrate.
5. Leave at room temperature for 4-5 days.
6. Dry and make powder for Wagu.
7. Place the peeled plums in cal x bath and rotate every 5 minutes to avoid the cal sitting on the bottom.
8. Rinse after 1 hour but retain bath for re-use.
9. Vacuum pack with 60% sugar syrup with fresh yuzu (if in season).
10. Cook in oven until soft (approx. 90 mins).
11. Stand up tight and dehydrate until desired texture (2-3 hours at 55 degrees).
12. Using tweezers, remove the stone from the plums.

Other details:
finishing_and_plating: Agar Blossom Sugar, Extra syrup 10g agar blossom

name: Black Sesame Custard Bun
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_3807_parsed
Name: Black Sesame Custard Bun

Ingredients:
- For the brioche
- 500g plain flour
- 40g sugar
- 15g whole egg
- 150g milk
- 20g yeast
- 3g carbon black
- For the sesame cream
- 85g sugar
- 15g corn flour
- 150g milk
- 100g whole egg
- 70g dark chocolate
- 40g sesame paste

Steps:
1. Warm the milk to 37°C, add the yeast and sugar and carbon.
2. In the mixing bowl with the hook, mix the flour and the milk.
3. Once a dough has formed add the egg.
4. Add the cold butter to the dough a little bit at a time.
5. Once butter incorporated add the salt and mix on medium speed 5 minutes until dough is smooth.
6. Leave to rest, weigh out 30g balls.
7. Whisk together the sugar, yolks and corn flour.
8. Warm the milk and combine with yolk mixture.
9. Cook to 82°C.
10. Once cooked pour the mixture over the chocolate and emulsify, then add sesame paste and allow to cool in the fridge.
11. Once in pipe form, split into small spheres at 25g each.
12. Freeze and demould and store in freezer.
13. Cover the frozen custard with 60% dough from earlier.
14. Prove in the oven at 30°C for 30 minutes with 60% humidity.
15. Once proved, bake at 190°C and cook for 8 minutes.
16. Remove cool and store in the fridge.

Other details:
temperature: Bake at 190°C
cook_time: 8 minutes
rest_time: 30 minutes proving
servings: 30g balls

name: Chucrut
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_2087_parsed
Name: Chucrut

Ingredients:
- Col de Benza
- 2% sal gruesa

Steps:
1. Cortar la col en la maquina cortafiambres muy fina.
2. Anadir la sal y mezclar bien.
3. Servir en una bandeja y prensar poniendo peso encima.
4. Fermentar durante un minimo de 4 semanas.
5. Extraer la col fermentada y lavar el exceso de sal con agua si es necesario.
6. Secar el sauerkraut durante 48 horas a 60°C aprox.
7. Triturar el sauerkraut seco en thermomix y tamizar hasta obtener un polvo agrio y salado.

Other details:

name: White Chocolate and Matcha
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_3787_parsed
Name: White Chocolate and Matcha

Ingredients:
- 130 ml water
- 20 g matcha
- 0.25 g citric acid
- 0.5 g salt
- 130 g base gold gelatin
- 180 g white chocolate
- 450 g whipped cream
- 72 g milk chocolate
- 340 g butter
- 258 g flour
- 258 g sugar
- 2.5 g salt

Steps:
1. Infuse water and lesser quality matcha, salt and citric acid for 5 minutes.
2. Pass.
3. Heat base and add gelatin.
4. Add good matcha and blitz.
5. Pour over white chocolate and blitz.
6. When cool fold in semi whipped cream.
7. Combine and blitz with a hand blender to form bubbles.
8. Immediately lined plastic box and blast chill.
9. Keep in the last chiller for service.
10. Using the Kenwood cream the butter and sugar.
11. Add the flour and bake in the oven for 15 minutes.
12. Spread on a Silpat and bake in the oven for 15 minutes at 160 degrees with low humidity and fan.
13. Freeze and blitz.
14. Roast the white chocolate in the oven at 160 degrees for 12 minutes.
15. Keep in the freezer.
16. Mix together and add 40 g white chocolate to make the powder ready for service.

Other details:

Heat base and add gelatin.

name: Pastel de arroz negro fermentado con koshu
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_1502_parsed
Name: Pastel de arroz negro fermentado con koshu

Ingredients:
- 300 g arroz Yume Nishiki
- 210 ml agua
- 2 g koji-kin

Steps:
1. Lavar el arroz hasta que el agua salga limpia. Dejar junto al agua durante 30 minutos.
2. Escurrir el exceso de agua y cocer el arroz durante 37 minutos en programa 'white rice'.
3. Deshidratar las bandejas para esta preparacion.
4. Espolvorear el koji-kin de forma uniforme encima del arroz.
5. Dejar fermentar el arroz con koji-kin durante 24 horas a 32°C.
6. Remover el arroz cada 12 horas.
7. Guardar el arroz envuelto en el frigorifico para detener la fermentacion.

Other details:
servings: 2 personas
temperature: 32°C
fermentation_time: 24 horas

name: Lobster and Pistachio
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_2136_parsed
Name: Lobster and Pistachio

Ingredients:
- Lobster shells
- Olive oil
- 1500ml fish stock
- 200g pistachios
- 400g rice vinegar
- 80g garlic
- 500g leeks
- 200g white onion

Steps:
1. Store under wet towel in the fridge.
2. Cook per day.
3. Break lobster at head. Reserve coral (if any).
4. Vacuum freeze or store accordingly to make oil.
5. Freeze heads or store under pressure and push through half the shell of the lobster.
6. Dip tail in water at 74 degrees for 9-12 minutes.
7. Tip: Should be portion as per lobster service.
8. Store outside fridge on a tray, cling filmed and protected.
9. Roast shells.
10. Blend with oil and coral.
11. Freeze.
12. Finely chop the garlic, leeks, and onion, then sweat them in a little olive oil without browning.
13. Add the vinegar and reduce. Once reduced, add the fish stock and cook for another 2 minutes.
14. Finally add the vegetable stock and pistachio and bring to the boil.
15. Remove from heat and blend to a smooth.
16. Season with salt.

Other details:
temperature: 74 degrees
cook_time: 9-12 minutes

name: Horseradish Cream
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_5728_parsed
Name: Horseradish Cream

Ingredients:
- 100g horseradish juice
- 1200g buttermilk
- 300g milk
- 35g lemon juice
- 275g milk
- 275g cream
- 36g cornflour
- 15g salt

Steps:
1. Add horseradish juice to stage 1 and chill over ice bath.
2. For stage 2:
3. Mix cornflour and milk in bowl.
4. Bring cream to the boil.
5. Pour milk and cornflour into cream and cook out for 5 minutes.
6. Pour mix both together over the ice bath and hand blend in the salt.
7. Check with senior chef.
8. Store in medium bags @ 500g each.
9. Freeze flat.
10. Blitz fresh everyday.

Other details:

raw.name: Horseradish Cream

For stage 2:
Mix cornflour and milk in bowl.
Bring cream to the boil.
Pour milk and cornflour into cream and cook out for 5 minutes.
Pour mix both together over the ice bath and hand blend in the salt.
Check with senior chef.
Store in medium bags @ 500g each.
Freeze flat.
Blitz fresh everyday.

name: Taro Soup
text:
Recipe ID: Taro Soup Base_Sheet1_0_parsed
Name: Taro Soup

Ingredients:
- 25 g Ginger
- 15 g Garlic
- 10 g Oil
- 300 g Taro Cubes
- 600 g Chicken Stock
- 30 g Oyster Sauce
- 10 g Light Soy

Steps:
1. Cut the taro into cubes and keep the trimmings.
2. In a pan, toast the ginger and garlic in the oil. Deglaze with oyster sauce, soy, and chicken stock.
3. Cook the trimmings with this for 30 minutes on a light simmer.
4. Strain this and discard the cooking trimmings.
5. Cook the cubed taro in the taro stock gently for 3-5 minutes until the taro is just cooked but not mushy.
6. Blast chill straight away to not overcook the taro.

Other details:
notes: Pay attention to the cooking of the taro cubes, not raw, not mushy.

name: Ananas al kirsch
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_5699_parsed
Name: Ananas al kirsch

Ingredients:
- 500g ananas
- 400g sciroppo
- 110g zucch. caramellato
- pere
- 150g acqua
- 90g zucch.
- 400g pera a pezzi
- 400g pera per pezzi
- 70g cioccolato
- 9 uova
- 270g ciocc 65%
- 200g zucch.
- 170g zucch.

Steps:
1. Bollire e versa sull'ananas
2. Caramella zucch. + glucosio, versa l'acqua poi il resto, bollire 3 m.
3. Cuoc. 160° 35m.
4. Come pasta all'uovo
5. E rip. 1h.
6. Scalda la panna + gelatina poi il panettone e la forma e congela
7. Albumi + zucch. poi i tuorli e il cacao a 30x50 165° 15m.
8. Monta tuorli + zucch. Aggi la cioccolata fusa e il resto
9. Monta gelare
10. Versa sul ciocc. montata e gela
11. Bollire tutto e filtrare
12. Cuoci a 85% + succo ar. E monta finendo poi il resto e la panna
13. Senza tuorli
14. X 3 st. liquori
15. Bollire panna glu
16. Cosi. coniando il

name: Spring Xian Bing
text:
Recipe ID: Spring Xian Bing_Sheet1_0_parsed
Name: Spring Xian Bing

Ingredients:
- 200 g Glass noodle
- 500 g Dashi
- 20 g Maldon salt
- 40 g Cooked Glass noodles
- 50 g Roasted fennel
- 30 g Dill
- 30 g Wild garlic
- 20 g Spring onion
- 0.5 tsp Green sichuan oil 青花椒油
- 10 g Aged White Soy屏東白醬油
- 10 g Tong choi (magic cabbage)
- 0.5 tsp Kow Choi Oil
- 0.25 tsp White Pepper

Steps:
1. Simmer glass noodles for 5min until cooked but still firm.
2. Strain the dashi and keep the liquid.
3. Rinse the glass noodles under cold running water to stop it from cooking further.
4. Cut the fennel to cube size, oil it gently.
5. Roast the cubed fennel in josper for about 5min, keep an eye on it.
6. Use a scissors to cut down the cooked glass noodles to 2cm lengths.
7. Chop the spring onion to very fine rings.
8. Slice the wild garlic really thin, and about 3cm long.
9. Chop the dills to 2cm wide, do not include the end stems.
10. In a mixing bowl, put in the weighed glass noodles.
11. Season the glass noodles with Aged white soy, white pepper, kow choi oil and green sichuan oil.
12. Taste, add a pinch of salt if it needs it.
13. Roll and pan fry like xian bing and serve with salted plum juice mirin.

Other details:
notes: ["Make fresh batch everyday. DO NOT use any leftovers overnight. It's a spring xian bing, it needs to be fresh!!!!", 'Always make small batch only.', 'Glass noodles should always be 30% of the total herbs weight.']
serve_with: Salted plum juice and mirin dip

Always make small batch only.

text:
Recipe ID: IMG_2104_parsed
Name: 

Ingredients:
- 12 'quesos' (Galapagos)
- 1 uva, 'queso de al-'

Steps:
1. Sobre una estera con un poco de agua disponer los 'quesos'.
2. Bajar la plancha a 50 grados y dejar que se fundan 5 minutos.
3. Sobre cada 'queso' echar agua durante 3 segundos.
4. Hacer rodar la bandeja cada dia para mantener la humedad.
5. Cuando los 'quesos' esten fermentados, con mucho cuidado, sin manchar la piel guardarlos en nevera.
6. Ahumar los 'quesos' con sarmiento, tomillo y romero durante 12 minutos.
7. Calentar el queso en microondas, baja potencia.
8. Romper el brioche con las manos y marcar los trozos.
9. Servir las raciones de brioche en una tela de pan cafe y con un queso entero para cortar en sala.

Other details:
author: Mugaritz 2017

name: Fish Stock Recipe
text:
Recipe ID: cuttlefish ball mix_Sheet1_0_parsed
Name: Fish Stock Recipe

Ingredients:
- 10 Seabass Carcasses
- 8 Spring Onion
- 2 Ginger Peeled Medium

Steps:
1. Make 10% salt water brine. Take the gill off from all the fish. Clean fish and place in salty brine water for 30 minutes.
2. Prepare a big pot of boiling water in the meantime.
3. Take out the carcass from brine water and blanch them for 10 seconds, do 3 carcasses at a time.
4. Once done, take out and place in cold water, then wash blood out from spine and all scum from inside.
5. Do not leave them in running water, just wash quickly and immediately drain.
6. While doing the above process, prepare another stock pot with 10 liters of filtered water and bring to a boil.
7. Once water is boiling, place ginger and spring onion (smashed) and 10 fish carcasses in pot. Bring to a boil and simmer for about 1 hour.
8. The temperature of simmering should be enough just for bubbles coming up to the surface.
9. Keep skimming frequently and remove all dirt while cooking, as water circulates well, it extracts better taste.
10. Make sure you skim well and maintain correct temperature of stock for better and cleaner taste.
11. When your stock is ready and tastes right, take all ingredients out and pass through muslin cloth into another pot.
12. It seems very thin compared to western style stock, but that’s what you are looking for: clean light flavor.
13. Concentrate more on the cleanness and freshness of the taste through your gorge and nose.
14. Make sure you skim well and maintain correct temperature of stock for better and cleaner taste.
15. Remember, stock making is the basic of a chef in Asia, and it only needs your attention and care for food.

Other details:
total_time: 1 hour

Remember, stock making is the basic of a chef in Asia, and it only needs your attention and care for food.

name: Wagu and Salted Plum
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_3782_parsed
Name: Wagu and Salted Plum

Ingredients:
- Wagu
- Plum powder
- Mole mancho

Steps:
1. Clean
2. Prepare and portion into 100g
3. Temper the wagu before service
4. Set the circulator to 60 degrees
5. Sear on high heat until caramelized and then cook in rendered wagu fat
6. Place into rendered wagu fat
7. Cook until 38 degrees
8. Sear on high heat until caramelized and then carve against the grain into several slices
9. Brush with mole mancho
10. Peel plums with plum peeler
11. Kean, skin and lacto ferment with 2% salt
12. Leave at room temperature for 4-5 days
13. Dry and make powder for wagu
14. Pass powder to ensure it is fine
15. See recipe

Other details:

raw.name: Wagu and Salted Plum
steps: Clean
Prepare and portion into 100g
Temper the wagu before service
Set the circulator to 60 degrees
Sear on high heat until caramelized and then cook in rendered wagu fat
Place into rendered wagu fat
Cook until 38 degrees
Sear on high heat until caramelized and then carve against the grain into several slices
Brush with mole mancho
Peel plums with plum peeler
Kean, skin and lacto ferment with 2% salt
Leave at room temperature for 4-5 days
Dry and make powder for wagu
Pass powder to ensure it is fine
See recipe

name: Marinated Pineapple into Coriander, Ginger and Mint Syrup
text:
Recipe ID: IMG_5792_parsed
Name: Marinated Pineapple into Coriander, Ginger and Mint Syrup

Ingredients:
- Stock syrup: 600gr
- Water: 300gr
- Ginger juice: 40gr
- Chopped coriander: 15gr
- Half a large pineapple
- Base: 480gr
- Granny Smith juice: 1025gr
- Lime juice: 25gr
- Ascorbic acid: 1gr
- Coriander leaves: 12gr

Steps:
1. Mix all together and Vac-pack. (Label 5 day)
2. Cut the half pineapple with the skin with the ham-slicer at 1.5mm.
3. Vac-pack in the syrup with the chopped herbs.
4. Keep in the fridge. (Label 3 days)
5. Mix all the dry together, bring the water to 90°C and add the dry. Hand blend till fluid.
6. Bring to 90°C, pour in a container and cool down in the fridge. (Label 1 month)
7. Blitz the juice with a sorbetque and lime juice with the base on a Vitaprep.
8. When all the leaves are dissolved add by the rest of the sorbet to make sure the sorbet is smooth.
9. Pass through a chinois and in the ice cream machine straight away.
10. Store in Pacos and freeze. (Label 1 month)

Other details:
notes: Make at least 6 hours before use.
